name: Celanese Corp
business: Celanese is a global chemical and specialty materials company operating through Engineered Materials and Acetyl Chain segments. It produces high performance engineered polymers and acetyl products used in automotive, medical, consumer electronics, energy storage, construction, and other industries. The company's products enjoy leading global positions due to differentiated business models, large production capacity, and proprietary technology.
sellsTo: Original equipment manufacturers and their suppliers

exposures.sentence: Vamac EAE, our elastomer brand, is primarily used in variety of demanding automotive applications, including electric and hybrid vehicle components.
UHMW-PE is used in battery separators, industrial products, filtration equipment, coatings and medical applications.

VitalDose . Our EVA copolymers, sold under the VitalDose trademark, are an enabling technology used for controlled-release drugs, medical implants and combination devices, including drug-eluting implants, reliable controlled-release performance in subcutaneous and surgical implants, intravitreal and extraocular devices.
Primary end uses for LCP are electrical applications or products and consumer electronics.
These include composite technologies for the utility industry that deliver greater reliability, capacity and performance for utility transmission lines.

These products are used in a wide variety of automotive, electrical, medical, industrial and consumer applications, including ignition system parts, radiator grilles, electrical switches, medical devices, insulation, photovoltaic panels, critical energy components, appliance and sensor housings, light emitting diodes and technical fibers.

We have carbon capture and utilization ("CCU") operations at our Clear Lake site as part of our Fairway joint venture. The unit is capable of capturing CO2 industrial emissions and producing low-carbon methanol which would help our global customers meet the growing demand for more sustainable and circular solutions. The products were launched in 2025 under the ECO-CC name and are supported through mass balance tracking and life cycle assessment processes.

Our increasing reliance on artificial intelligence ("AI") technologies in our products, services, and operations presents risks that could adversely impact our business, financial condition, and results of operations.

catalysts.event: Completion of new liquid crystal polymer (LCP) plant in Nanjing, China
catalysts.sentence: the new liquid crystal polymer ("LCP") plant is in construction and on schedule for completion in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Start-up of expanded VAE emulsion plant in Frankfurt, Germany
catalysts.sentence: our planned expansion of our vinyl acetate ethylene ("VAE") emulsion plant in Frankfurt, Germany is in construction with start-up scheduled in the first half of 2026 to align with demand.

catalysts.event: Permanent cessation of manufacturing operations at Lanaken, Belgium facility
catalysts.sentence: We intend to permanently cease all manufacturing operations during the second half of 2026.
On February 2, 2026, we completed the sale of the Micromax business to Element Solutions Inc for a purchase price of $492 million, subject to customary transaction adjustments.
